name: Salesforce
description: Cloud CRM and application platform. Yields evidence of configuration change history (Setup Audit Trail), user access and login records, security posture scoring (Health Check) and, with Shield, detailed API and user activity logs.

steps.action: Navigate to Setup, enter 'View Setup Audit Trail' in the Quick Find box, then click 'Download setup audit trail for last six months' to export the CSV of configuration changes
steps.security_note: Use a least-privilege admin account for evidence collection, not a full System Administrator profile where avoidable
steps.audit_note: Setup Audit Trail entries are generated by Salesforce and cannot be edited; only the last six months are retained, so export at least twice a year to avoid gaps

steps.action: Navigate to Setup, enter 'Login History' in the Quick Find box, filter to the collection period and download the CSV of user and API logins
steps.audit_note: Login History covers the past six months and includes API logins; ensure consecutive exports overlap so no logins are missed

steps.action: Navigate to Setup, enter 'Health Check' in the Quick Find box and capture a dated screenshot of the Health Check score and any high-risk security settings
steps.permissions_required: View Setup and Configuration, Manage Password Policies
steps.audit_note: The Health Check score is a point-in-time measure against the Salesforce baseline; record the date and baseline used

steps.action: Navigate to Setup > Users > Profiles and Setup > Users > Permission Sets, and capture screenshots or a Users report export showing profile and permission set assignments for privileged users
steps.audit_note: Access review evidence should show who holds System Administrator and 'Modify All Data' style permissions and when it was captured

steps.action: Confirm Field History Tracking is enabled on in-scope objects (object's Fields & Relationships > Set History Tracking) and screenshot the tracked field list
steps.audit_note: Field history is retained for 18 months by default (24 months via the API); longer retention requires the Field Audit Trail add-on

steps.action: Upload the exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and the evidence ID reference
steps.security_note: Login History and audit trail exports contain user identities and IP addresses — treat as confidential and verify no credentials or personal data beyond what is needed are included

steps.action: Build custom reports using the Users report type (active users, profile, role, last login date) and permission set assignments to cover access review evidence
From each report's run page, click Subscribe and set the schedule to match the evidence collection frequency (e.g. weekly on Monday)
steps.audit_note: Subscription emails include the report run timestamp, providing evidence of a regular cadence

steps.action: In the subscription, click Attach File and select Report Details (.csv) so the results arrive as a file that can be filed directly as evidence
steps.security_note: Limit recipients to authorised compliance personnel — the attachment contains user and account data
steps.audit_note: Choose 'Run Report As' a stable integration or compliance user so results are consistent between runs

steps.action: File each delivered attachment in the evidence repository on receipt, retaining the delivery email as proof of the schedule
steps.audit_note: Keep a simple log of received runs so missed deliveries are noticed within one cycle

steps.action: Note that Setup Audit Trail and Login History cannot be delivered by report subscriptions (they are Setup pages, not report types) — continue the L1 manual export for those, or move to API collection (L3)
steps.audit_note: Document this known limitation in the collection procedure so auditors understand the mixed approach

steps.action: Create a connected app (or external client app, recommended from Spring '26) with 'Enable OAuth Settings' and 'Enable Client Credentials Flow', and assign a dedicated integration user as the execution user
steps.permissions_required: Customize Application, Manage Connected Apps; execution user needs API Enabled plus View Setup and Configuration
steps.security_note: Anyone holding the consumer key and secret can obtain access tokens — store both in a secrets manager, never in source control, and rotate the secret regularly

steps.action: Exchange the consumer key and secret for an access token via POST /services/oauth2/token with grant_type=client_credentials, then call the REST query endpoint
steps.security_note: Request tokens over TLS only and never log them; the execution user should hold read-only permissions

steps.action: Query audit objects daily via SOQL, e.g. GET /services/data/v61.0/query?q=SELECT Id,Action,Section,CreatedDate,CreatedById FROM SetupAuditTrail WHERE CreatedDate = LAST_N_DAYS:1, and similarly LoginHistory for authentication evidence
steps.permissions_required: View Setup and Configuration (SetupAuditTrail), Manage Users (LoginHistory)
steps.audit_note: Daily incremental queries avoid the six-month retention cliff on both Setup Audit Trail and Login History

steps.action: If the org has Salesforce Shield or the Event Monitoring add-on, query EventLogFile (e.g. SELECT Id,EventType,LogDate,LogFile FROM EventLogFile WHERE EventType = 'API' AND LogDate = YESTERDAY) and download the LogFile blob for API access evidence; without Shield this object is not available
steps.permissions_required: View Event Log Files, API Enabled
steps.audit_note: Event log files are generated by Salesforce and retained for up to one year with the add-on — state clearly in the procedure whether Shield is licensed

steps.action: Schedule the collection script via cron or a CI/CD pipeline to run daily, writing date-stamped exports to the evidence repository, with error handling that alerts on failed runs
steps.security_note: Run the job from a hardened runner; credentials come from the secrets manager at runtime only
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should trigger re-collection within an agreed SLA and be recorded

steps.action: Validate each run against expected volumes (e.g. daily login count and setup-change count vs a rolling baseline) and flag anomalies for review
steps.audit_note: Volume validation demonstrates collection completeness to auditors

L4.title: Salesforce Managed Evidence Pipeline with Real-Time Event Monitoring

steps.action: With Salesforce Shield or the Event Monitoring add-on, enable Real-Time Event Monitoring (Setup > Event Manager) and select events to stream and store, e.g. LoginEventStream, ApiEventStream and PermissionSetEventStream
steps.permissions_required: View Real-Time Event Monitoring Data, Customize Application
steps.audit_note: This capability requires the Shield/Event Monitoring add-on — without it, the closest alternative is the daily EventLogFile collection described in L3

steps.action: Subscribe your SIEM or evidence platform to the event streams via the Pub/Sub API or CometD, and enable big object storage so events are also retained in-org for six months
steps.permissions_required: Integration user with API Enabled and View Real-Time Event Monitoring Data
steps.security_note: Stream over TLS 1.2+ using the least-privileged integration user; streamed events include user identities and IP addresses

steps.action: Reconcile the stream daily against EventLogFile counts to detect missed events, and keep the L3 SetupAuditTrail query as an independent completeness check
steps.audit_note: Cross-source reconciliation is strong evidence that the collection process is managed rather than best-effort

steps.action: Track collection completeness metrics — events per day by type, ingest latency, coverage of in-scope event types — and alert when volume drops below baseline or latency exceeds threshold
Publish a collection health dashboard (events/day, p95 latency, coverage %, failed runs) and generate an automated quarterly collection health report flagging any SLA breaches
steps.audit_note: The dashboard and quarterly report serve as continuous evidence of process health
